reasoning:
In May 2025, the Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS) rescinded the January 2025 AI Diffusion Interim Final Rule (which established ECCN 4E091 on model weights), citing concerns over AI innovation and allied impacts. However, alongside the rescission, BIS explicitly announced it would issue a "replacement rule in the near future" and indicated it may skip long public comment periods to accelerate implementation. Additionally, on May 13, BIS issued new guidance and policy statements outlining expanded licensing requirements under Part 744 for IaaS (cloud) providers and advanced computing items, particularly regarding end-uses in Country Group D:5 (China, etc.). 

If the promised replacement rule is issued, it will likely count as a "modification of the controls implemented under ECCN 4E091," satisfying the criteria. Even if the replacement rule applies slightly weaker or more targeted controls, this counts as a modification. As of early August, no new rule has appeared in the Federal Register, which explains why the timeline is shrinking and probability should not be overwhelming. But given the clear commitment to a replacement rule, the ongoing focus on China, the explicitly stated intention to bypass public comment periods, and the 5 months remaining in the year, the likelihood of a new Interim Final Rule or Final Rule taking effect before December 31, 2025, remains solid. The crowd was at 60% in late July. A slight downward adjustment to 55% respects the passage of time whilst recognizing the heavily signaled impending rule.
